data.content:
### Install Dependencies

Source: https://github.com/facebook/lexical/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md

Installs the necessary project dependencies using npm.

```bash
npm install
```

--------------------------------

### Lexical ESLint Plugin package.json

Source: https://github.com/facebook/lexical/blob/main/packages/lexical-website/docs/maintainers-guide.md

The package.json configuration file for the lexical-eslint-plugin. It includes metadata like name, version, license, repository details, and entry points for the package.

```json
{
  "name": "@lexical/eslint-plugin",
  "description": "",
  "keywords": [
    "lexical",
    "editor"
  ],
  "version": "0.14.3",
  "license": "MIT",
  "repository": {
    "type": "git",
    "url": "git+https://github.com/facebook/lexical.git",
    "directory": "packages/lexical-eslint-plugin"
  },
  "main": "LexicalEslintPlugin.js",
  "types": "index.d.ts",
  "bugs": {
    "url": "https://github.com/facebook/lexical/issues"
  },
  "homepage": "https://github.com/facebook/lexical#readme"
}
```

--------------------------------

### Install Project Dependencies

Source: https://github.com/facebook/lexical/blob/main/packages/lexical-website/README.md

Installs all project dependencies required for local development and building the website. This command should be run after cloning the repository.

```bash
$ yarn
```

--------------------------------

### Install @lexical/eslint-plugin

Source: https://github.com/facebook/lexical/blob/main/packages/lexical-eslint-plugin/README.md

Installs the @lexical/eslint-plugin package as a development dependency using npm.

```sh
npm install @lexical/eslint-plugin --save-dev
```

--------------------------------

### TextNode with updated version

Source: https://github.com/facebook/lexical/blob/main/packages/lexical-website/docs/concepts/serialization.md

Shows an updated TextNode class with a new version number in its exportJSON method.

```typescript
class TextNode {
  exportJSON() {
    return { /* ... */, version: 2 };
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Versioning & Breaking Changes

Source: https://github.com/facebook/lexical/blob/main/packages/lexical-website/docs/concepts/serialization.md

Recommendations for managing breaking changes in JSON objects and using version fields for custom nodes to maintain backwards compatibility.

```APIDOC
## Versioning & Breaking Changes

### Description
It's important to avoid making breaking changes to existing fields in your JSON object, especially if backwards compatibility is crucial. Use a version field to separate different changes in your node as you add or change functionality.

### Example of Serialized Type Definition
```typescript
import type {Spread} from 'lexical';

export type SerializedTextNode = Spread<
  {
    detail: number;
    format: number;
    mode: TextModeType;
    style: string;
    text: string;
  },
  SerializedLexicalNode
>;
```

### Adding New Fields Safely
To make changes without breaking compatibility, add new functionality as new optional property fields.

```typescript
export type SerializedTextNode = Spread<
  {
    detail: number;
    format: number;
    mode: TextModeType;
    style: string;
    text: string;
    // Our new field we've added
    newField?: string,
  },
  SerializedLexicalNode
>;
```
```

--------------------------------

### ExtendedTextNode overriding version

Source: https://github.com/facebook/lexical/blob/main/packages/lexical-website/docs/concepts/serialization.md

Presents an ExtendedTextNode that explicitly sets its own version, potentially overriding the base class's version information.

```typescript
class ExtendedTextNode extends TextNode {
  exportJSON() {
    // The super's layout has changed, but the version information is lost
    return { ...super.exportJSON(), version: 1 };
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Install Lexical and @lexical/react

Source: https://github.com/facebook/lexical/blob/main/packages/lexical-react/README.md

Installs the necessary Lexical and @lexical/react packages using npm. These are the core dependencies for building Lexical editors in React.

```bash
npm install --save lexical @lexical/react
```

--------------------------------

### TextNode exportJSON Example

Source: https://github.com/facebook/lexical/blob/main/packages/lexical-website/docs/concepts/serialization.md

Demonstrates a basic exportJSON method in a TextNode class, returning a JSON object with a version property.

```typescript
class TextNode {
  exportJSON() {
    return { /* ... */, version: 1 };
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Install Lexical React Collaboration Dependencies

Source: https://github.com/facebook/lexical/blob/main/packages/lexical-website/docs/collaboration/react.md

Installs the necessary Lexical React and Yjs packages for building collaborative editors. Includes Lexical core, React integration, Yjs bindings, and WebSocket provider.

```bash
npm i -S @lexical/react @lexical/yjs lexical react react-dom y-websocket yjs
```

--------------------------------

### Refactor React Plugin to React-Independent Extension (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/facebook/lexical/blob/main/packages/lexical-website/docs/extensions/react.md

Illustrates the transformation of a React-dependent plugin (CheckListPlugin) into a React-independent Lexical extension (CheckListExtension). This removes the React dependency by directly registering the core functionality.

```tsx
export function CheckListPlugin(): null {
  const [editor] = useLexicalComposerContext();
  useEffect(() => {
    return registerCheckList(editor);
  }, [editor]);
  return null;
}

export const CheckListExtension = defineExtension({
  name: "@lexical/list/CheckList",
  register: registerCheckList,
});
```

--------------------------------

### Use RichTextExtension with LexicalExtensionComposer

Source: https://github.com/facebook/lexical/blob/main/packages/lexical-website/docs/extensions/react.md

This snippet demonstrates how to integrate `RichTextExtension` as a dependency within a Lexical `extension`, simplifying the composer setup. Instead of directly using `RichTextPlugin`, `RichTextExtension` is declared in the `dependencies` array.

```tsx
const appExtension = defineExtension({
  name: 'MyEditor',
  namespace: 'MyEditor',
  dependencies: [RichTextExtension],
  theme,
  $initialEditorState,
});

<LexicalExtensionComposer extension={appExtension}>
  {/* By default the contentEditable is the first child, defaulting to <ContentEditable /> */}
  {/* other legacy React plugins */}
</LexicalExtensionComposer>
```

--------------------------------

### Declare Lexical Extension Peer Dependency

Source: https://github.com/facebook/lexical/blob/main/packages/lexical-website/docs/extensions/intro.md

Declares an indirect dependency on another Lexical extension by its name. This is used to manage dependencies between extensions, ensuring that required extensions are available for a given editor setup. It helps in building complex editor functionalities that rely on other components.

```typescript
import { declarePeerDependency } from "lexical";

declarePeerDependency("AnotherExtensionName");
```

--------------------------------

### Configuring Extension Dependencies with References and Configs

Source: https://github.com/facebook/lexical/blob/main/packages/lexical-website/docs/extensions/defining-extensions.md

This TypeScript example illustrates defining an extension named '@example/extension' that depends on other extensions. It showcases using direct references ('SomeExtension') and the 'configExtension' utility to provide specific configurations (e.g., decorators for 'ReactExtension').

```typescript
export const ExampleExtension = defineExtension({
  name: "@example/extension",
  dependencies: [
    SomeExtension,
    configExtension(ReactExtension, {
      decorators: [<ExampleDecorator />]
    }),
  ],
});
```

--------------------------------

### Create Lexical ESLint Plugin Workspace

Source: https://github.com/facebook/lexical/blob/main/packages/lexical-website/docs/maintainers-guide.md

Command to initialize a new npm workspace for the lexical-eslint-plugin within the monorepo. This command sets up the initial package.json file for the new package.

```bash
npm init -w packages/lexical-eslint-plugin
```

--------------------------------

### Build Svelte Project for Production with npm

Source: https://github.com/facebook/lexical/blob/main/scripts/__tests__/integration/fixtures/lexical-esm-sveltekit-vanilla-js/README.md

Command to build a production-ready version of your Svelte application. This command optimizes your code for deployment. Previewing the build is possible with 'npm run preview'.

```bash
npm run build
```

--------------------------------

### Create Astro Project with Basics Template

Source: https://github.com/facebook/lexical/blob/main/scripts/__tests__/integration/fixtures/lexical-esm-astro-react/README.md

Use this command to create a new Astro project initialized with the 'Basics' template. This command will prompt you to choose a template and set up a new Astro project in your current directory. It does not require any additional dependencies beyond Node.js and npm.

```sh
npm create astro@latest -- --template basics
```

--------------------------------

### LexicalEditorRefPlugin: Get Editor Instance Ref

Source: https://github.com/facebook/lexical/blob/main/packages/lexical-website/docs/react/plugins.md

Provides a ref to the underlying Lexical editor instance, allowing interaction with the editor from outside the LexicalComposer. Dependencies: Lexical, React.useRef.

```jsx
const editorRef = useRef(null);
<EditorRefPlugin editorRef={editorRef} />;
```

--------------------------------

### Safari Extension Development and Archiving (Bash)

Source: https://github.com/facebook/lexical/blob/main/packages/lexical-devtools/README.md

Commands for developing and archiving the Safari version of the Lexical DevTools extension. Requires Xcode installation and environment setup. Includes commands for building and uploading the archive.

```bash
# Environment setup
sudo xcode-select -s /Applications/Xcode.app
xcodebuild --install
sudo xcodebuild -license
xcodebuild -runFirstLaunch

# Normal operation
npm run dev:safari

# Build & upload to Apple Connect
BUILD_VERSION=0 npm run safari:archive 
PASSWORD="XXX" npm run safari:upload
```

--------------------------------

### Develop Svelte Project with npm

Source: https://github.com/facebook/lexical/blob/main/scripts/__tests__/integration/fixtures/lexical-esm-sveltekit-vanilla-js/README.md

Commands to start the development server for a Svelte project. After installing dependencies, these commands will launch a local server to view your application. The '-- --open' flag will automatically open the app in a new browser tab.

```bash
npm run dev

# or start the server and open the app in a new browser tab
npm run dev -- --open
```

--------------------------------

### Astro Project Commands

Source: https://github.com/facebook/lexical/blob/main/scripts/__tests__/integration/fixtures/lexical-esm-astro-react/README.md

These commands are used to manage the development and building process of an Astro project. They are executed from the root of the project in a terminal and cover dependency installation, local development server startup, production builds, and previewing builds.

```sh
npm install
npm run dev
npm run build
npm run preview
npm run astro ...
npm run astro -- --help
```
